prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French: Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n’importe quelle dispute aurait pu en dire.

Japanese: 私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな言い争いよりも多くを物語っていた。

Arabic: لقد قال الصمت بيننا أكثرَ مما كان بإمكان أيّ جدالٍ أن يقوله.

Notes on tricky parts:
- “Argument”: In English it’s ambiguous (“reason” vs “quarrel”). Chosen as “dispute/quarrel”: French dispute, Japanese 言い争い, Arabic جدال.
- “Said” applied to “silence”: Anthropomorphic in English. Japanese uses 物語っていた (“told/related”), Arabic keeps “قال” but this could also be rendered as “كان أبلغ” (“was more eloquent”) depending on style.
- “Could have”: Rendered with French conditional perfect (aurait pu), Arabic “كان بإمكان … أن …”; Japanese conveys the modality via comparison (よりも) rather than an explicit “could have.”
